name: Get-ScriptAnalyzerRule
-CustomRulePath
-CustomizedRulePath
options.description: By default, PSScriptAnalyzer gets only the standard rules specified in the `Microsoft.Windows.PowerShell.ScriptAnalyzer.BuiltinRules.dll` file in the module. Use this parameter to get the custom Script Analyzer rules in the specified path and the standard Script Analyzer rules.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Gets only rules with the specified names or name patterns. Wildcards are supported. If you list multiple names or patterns, it gets all rules that match any of the name patterns.

options.name: -RecurseCustomRulePath
options.description: Searches the CustomRulePath location recursively to add rules defined in files in subdirectories of the path. By default, `Get-ScriptAnalyzerRule` adds only the custom rules in the specified path.

options.name: -Severity
options.description: Gets only rules with the specified severity values. Valid values are:

description: Gets the script analyzer rules on the local computer.
